first_paragraph: The **Slovenian Catholic Church**, or **Catholic Church in Slovenia** ([Slovene](/wiki/Slovene_language "Slovene language"): *Katoliška cerkev v Sloveniji*) is part of the worldwide [Catholic Church](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church"), under the spiritual leadership of the [Pope](/wiki/Pope "Pope") in [Rome](/wiki/Rome "Rome"). The 2018 Eurobarometer data shows 73.4% of Slovenian population identifying as Catholic[[1]](#cite_note-EB2018-1) that fell to 72.1% in the 2019 Eurobarometer survey.[[2]](#cite_note-EB2019-2) According to the Catholic Church data, the Catholic population fell from 78.04% in 2009 to 72.11% in 2019.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

The **Slovenian Catholic Church**, or **Catholic Church in Slovenia** ([Slovene](/wiki/Slovene_language "Slovene language"): *Katoliška cerkev v Sloveniji*) is part of the worldwide [Catholic Church](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church"), under the spiritual leadership of the [Pope](/wiki/Pope "Pope") in [Rome](/wiki/Rome "Rome"). The 2018 Eurobarometer data shows 73.4% of Slovenian population identifying as Catholic[[1]](#cite_note-EB2018-1) that fell to 72.1% in the 2019 Eurobarometer survey.[[2]](#cite_note-EB2019-2) According to the Catholic Church data, the Catholic population fell from 78.04% in 2009 to 72.11% in 2019.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

There are total of 1,509,986 (72.11%) Catholics in Slovenia in 2019 by official statistics published by Catholic Church of Slovenia.[[4]](#cite_note-4) The country is divided into six [dioceses](/wiki/Diocese "Diocese"), including two [archdioceses](/wiki/Archdiocese "Archdiocese"). The diocese of [Maribor](/wiki/Maribor "Maribor") was elevated to an archdiocese by [Pope Benedict XVI](/wiki/Pope_Benedict_XVI "Pope Benedict XVI") in 2006. Additionally, the pope created three new sees, namely [Novo Mesto](/wiki/Novo_Mesto "Novo Mesto"), [Celje](/wiki/Celje "Celje") and [Murska Sobota](/wiki/Murska_Sobota "Murska Sobota").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a1/Cardinal_Franc_Rode.jpg/250px-Cardinal_Franc_Rode.jpg)](/wiki/File:Cardinal_Franc_Rode.jpg)

Cardinal Franc Rode CM

Slovenian Catholic Church is governed by the [Slovenian Bishops' Conference.](/wiki/Episcopal_Conference_of_Slovenia "Episcopal Conference of Slovenia") Informally archbishop of Ljubljana is considered Primate of Slovenia.

Slovenian Catholic Church and Slovenian priests operate in Slovenian churches worldwide, most notably in [Slovenian Church of St. Cyril in New York](/wiki/Slovenian_Church_of_St._Cyril_in_New_York "Slovenian Church of St. Cyril in New York").[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

Archbishop [Luigi Bianco](/wiki/Luigi_Bianco "Luigi Bianco"), [Titular](/wiki/Titular_Archbishop "Titular Archbishop") of [Falerone](/wiki/Falerone "Falerone"), is the [Apostolic Nuncio to Slovenia](/wiki/Apostolic_Nunciature_to_Slovenia "Apostolic Nunciature to Slovenia")[[7]](#cite_note-CH-7) and the [Apostolic Delegate](/wiki/Apostolic_Delegate "Apostolic Delegate") to [Kosovo](/wiki/Kosovo "Kosovo").

Cardinal [Franc Rode](/wiki/Franc_Rode "Franc Rode") CM, is the only cardinal of the Catholic Church in Slovenia. Another cardinal of Slovenin descent is [Vicente Bokalic Iglic](/wiki/Vicente_Bokalic_Iglic "Vicente Bokalic Iglic") CM of Argentina.

## Territorial organization

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Catholic_Church_in_Slovenia&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Territorial organization")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/9d/Slovenian_dioceses_of_Catholic_Church.png/250px-Slovenian_dioceses_of_Catholic_Church.png)](/wiki/File:Slovenian_dioceses_of_Catholic_Church.png)

Map of Slovenian dioceses: Ljubljana (1), Koper (2), Novo mesto (3), Maribor (4), Celje (5), Murska Sobota (6)

### Ecclesiastical structure

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Catholic_Church_in_Slovenia&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Ecclesiastical structure")]

[Catholic Church](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church") in [Slovenia](/wiki/Slovenia "Slovenia") is organized in two [ecclesiastical provinces](/wiki/Ecclesiastical_province "Ecclesiastical province") ([metropolises](/wiki/Metropolis_(religious_jurisdiction) "Metropolis (religious jurisdiction)"); *cerkvene pokrajine - metropolije*):

* Metropolis of Ljubljana (*Ljubljanska metropolija*), and
* Metropolis of Maribor (*Mariborska metropolija*).[[9]](#cite_note-:0-9)

Each metropolis is divided into three dioceses of which one is [metropolitan archdiocese](/wiki/Metropolitan_archdiocese "Metropolitan archdiocese") and two are [suffragan dioceses](/wiki/Suffragan_diocese "Suffragan diocese").[[9]](#cite_note-:0-9)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/66/Wappen_Bistum_Laibach.png/250px-Wappen_Bistum_Laibach.png)](/wiki/File:Wappen_Bistum_Laibach.png)

Coat of Arms of Archdiocese of Ljubljana

### Metropolis of Ljubljana

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Catholic_Church_in_Slovenia&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Metropolis of Ljubljana")]

Metropolis of Ljubljana is presided over by [Metropolitan](/wiki/Metropolitan_bishop "Metropolitan bishop") [Archbishop of Ljubljana](/wiki/Archbishop_of_Ljubljana "Archbishop of Ljubljana"), currently [Stanislav Zore](/wiki/Stanislav_Zore "Stanislav Zore") OFM.[[10]](#cite_note-:1-10)

It is divided into three dioceses:

* [Archdiocese of Ljubljana](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Archdiocese_of_Ljubljana "Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Ljubljana") (metropolitan see),
* [Diocese of Koper](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Diocese_of_Koper "Roman Catholic Diocese of Koper") (suffragan diocese),
* [Diocese of Novo mesto](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Diocese_of_Novo_Mesto "Roman Catholic Diocese of Novo Mesto") (suffragan diocese).[[10]](#cite_note-:1-10)

| Diocese[[10]](#cite_note-:1-10) | Bishop | Cathedral | Population | Catholics | Decanates | Parishes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [Ljubljana](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Archdiocese_of_Ljubljana "Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Ljubljana") | [Stanislav Zore](/wiki/Stanislav_Zore "Stanislav Zore") OFM | [Cathedral of Saint Nicholas](/wiki/Ljubljana_Cathedral "Ljubljana Cathedral") | 763.765 | 554.950 | 17 | 232 |
| [Koper](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Diocese_of_Koper "Roman Catholic Diocese of Koper") | [Peter Štumpf](/wiki/Peter_%C5%A0tumpf "Peter Štumpf") SDB, PhD | [Cathedral of the Assumption of the Blessed Virgin Mary](/wiki/Assumption_Cathedral,_Koper "Assumption Cathedral, Koper") | 258.380 | 202.900 | 11 | 189 |
| [Novo mesto](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Diocese_of_Novo_Mesto "Roman Catholic Diocese of Novo Mesto") | [Andrej Saje](/wiki/Andrej_Saje "Andrej Saje"), PhD | [Cathedral of Saint Nicholas](/wiki/Novo_Mesto_Cathedral "Novo Mesto Cathedral") | 159.600 | 137.500 | 6 | 71 |

Archdiocese of Ljubljana is divided into 4 [archdeaconries](/wiki/Archdeacon "Archdeacon") and 17 [deaneries](/wiki/Deanery "Deanery") (decanates):[[10]](#cite_note-:1-10)

* Archdeaconry I: Ljubljana – Center, Ljubljana – Moste, Ljubljana – Šentvid, Ljubljana – Vič/Rakovnik;
* Archdeaconry II: Kranj, Radovljica, Šenčur, Škofja Loka, Tržič;
* III. Archdeaconry III: Domžale, Kamnik, Litija, Zagorje; and
* Archdeaconry IV: Cerknica, Grosuplje, Ribnica, Vrhnika.

Decanates of Diocese of Koper are Dekani, Idrija – Cerkno, Ilirska Bistrica, Kobarid, Koper, Kraška dekanija, Nova Gorica, Postojna, Šempeter, Tolmin, Vipavska dekanija.[[10]](#cite_note-:1-10)

Decanates of Diocese of Novo mesto are Črnomelj, Kočevje, Leskovec, Novo mesto, Trebnje, Žužemberk.[[10]](#cite_note-:1-10)

### Metropolis of Maribor

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Catholic_Church_in_Slovenia&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Metropolis of Maribor")]

Metropolis of Maribor is presided over by [Metropolitan](/wiki/Metropolitan_bishop "Metropolitan bishop") [Archbishop of Maribor](/wiki/Archbishop_of_Maribor "Archbishop of Maribor"), currently [Alojzij Cvikl](/wiki/Alojzij_Cvikl "Alojzij Cvikl") SJ.

It is divided into three dioceses:

* [Archdiocese of Maribor](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Archdiocese_of_Maribor "Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Maribor") (metropolitan see),
* [Diocese of Celje](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Diocese_of_Celje "Roman Catholic Diocese of Celje") (suffragan diocese),
* [Diocese of Murska Sobota](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Diocese_of_Murska_Sobota "Roman Catholic Diocese of Murska Sobota") (suffragan diocese).[[11]](#cite_note-:2-11)

| Diocese[[11]](#cite_note-:2-11) | Bishop | Cathedral | Population | Catholics | [Decanates](/wiki/Deanery "Deanery") | [Parishes](/wiki/Parish "Parish") |

| [Maribor](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Archdiocese_of_Maribor "Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Maribor") | [Alojzij Cvikl](/wiki/Alojzij_Cvikl "Alojzij Cvikl") SJ | [Cathedral of Saint John the Baptist](/wiki/Maribor_Cathedral "Maribor Cathedral") | 419.055 | 355.654 | 12 | 143 |
| [Celje](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Diocese_of_Celje "Roman Catholic Diocese of Celje") | [Maksimilijan Matjaž](/wiki/Maksimilijan_Matja%C5%BE "Maksimilijan Matjaž"), ThD | [Cathedral of Saint Daniel](/wiki/Celje_Cathedral "Celje Cathedral") | 289.846 | 237.370 | 11 | 112 |
| [Murska Sobota](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Diocese_of_Murska_Sobota "Roman Catholic Diocese of Murska Sobota") | *[Peter Štumpf](/wiki/Peter_%C5%A0tumpf "Peter Štumpf") SDB, PhD, [Apostolic administrator](/wiki/Apostolic_administration "Apostolic administration") [sede vacante](/wiki/Sede_vacante "Sede vacante")* | [Cathedral of Saint Nicholas](/wiki/Murska_Sobota_Cathedral "Murska Sobota Cathedral") | 120.146 | 95.463 | 3 | 36 |

Archdiocese of Maribor is divided into 5 archdeaconries and 12 decanates:[[11]](#cite_note-:2-11)

* "Bistriško-konjiški" Archdeaconry: Slovenska Bistrica, Slovenske Konjice,
* "Mariborski" Archdeaconry: Maribor, Dravosko polje,
* "Ptujsko–ormoški" Archdeaconry: Ptuj, Velika Nedelja, Zavrč,
* "Slovenjegoriški" Archdeaconry: Jarenina, Lenart v Slovenskih goricah,
* "Koroški" Archdeaconry: Dravograd – Mežiška dolina, Radlje – Vuzenica, Stari trg.

Decanates of Diocese of Celje are Celje, Nova Cerkev, Žalec, Kozje, Rogatec, Šmarje pri Jelšah, Braslovče, Gornji Grad, Šaleška dolina, Laško, Videm ob Savi.[[11]](#cite_note-:2-11)

Decanates of Diocese of Murska Sobota are Lendava, Ljutomer, Murska Sobota.[[11]](#cite_note-:2-11)

## Judiciary

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Catholic_Church_in_Slovenia&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Judiciary")]

There are two [ecclesiastical courts](/wiki/Ecclesiastical_court "Ecclesiastical court") of first instance in Slovenia:

* Metropolitan Ecclesiastical Court in Ljubljana for Archdiocese of Ljubljana and dioceses of Koper and Novo mesto,
* Metropolitan Ecclesiastical Court in Maribor for Archdiocese of Maribor and dioceses of Celje and Murska Sobota.

Second instance courts are in Maribor (for Metropolis of Ljubljana) and Ljubljana (for Metropolis of Maribor).

The court of third instance operates within the Roman Curia and is called the [Roman Rota](/wiki/Roman_Rota "Roman Rota"). In addition, within the [Holy See](/wiki/Holy_See "Holy See"), there is the [Apostolic Signatura](/wiki/Apostolic_Signatura "Apostolic Signatura"), which is an administrative court responsible for assessing the validity of canonical legal procedures for the universal Church; it also has authority over the appointment of ecclesiastical judges in local courts. The third court within the universal Church is the [Apostolic Penitentiary](/wiki/Apostolic_Penitentiary "Apostolic Penitentiary"), which is responsible for matters concerning the internal sacramental forum of the Church. A tribunal has also been established within the [Dicastery for the Doctrine of the Faith](/wiki/Dicastery_for_the_Doctrine_of_the_Faith "Dicastery for the Doctrine of the Faith"), which deals with criminal offenses related to sexual abuse.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

## Relations with the Holy See

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Catholic_Church_in_Slovenia&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Relations with the Holy See")]

Slovenia and the [Holy See](/wiki/Holy_See "Holy See") concluded a [concordate](/wiki/Concordat "Concordat") on May 28, 2004.

The Holy See has a [apostolic nunciature in Ljubljana](/wiki/Apostolic_Nunciature_to_Slovenia "Apostolic Nunciature to Slovenia") and Slovenia has a separate embassy in Rome, responsible for the Holy See (additionally to the one for the Italian Republic). Archbishop [Luigi Bianco](/wiki/Luigi_Bianco "Luigi Bianco"), [Titular](/wiki/Titular_Archbishop "Titular Archbishop") of [Falerone](/wiki/Falerone "Falerone"), is the current [Apostolic Nuncio to Slovenia](/wiki/Apostolic_Nunciature_to_Slovenia "Apostolic Nunciature to Slovenia")[[7]](#cite_note-CH-7) and the [Apostolic Delegate](/wiki/Apostolic_Delegate "Apostolic Delegate") to [Kosovo](/wiki/Kosovo "Kosovo"). Franc But is an ambassador of Slovenia to the Holy See.

### Slovenians in the Roman Curia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Catholic_Church_in_Slovenia&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Slovenians in the Roman Curia")]

Cardinal [Franc Rode](/wiki/Franc_Rode "Franc Rode") CM served as Prefect of the [Congregation for Institutes of Consecrated Life and Societies of Apostolic Life](/wiki/Congregation_for_Institutes_of_Consecrated_Life_and_Societies_of_Apostolic_Life "Congregation for Institutes of Consecrated Life and Societies of Apostolic Life") from 2004 to 2011.

Archbishops [Ivan Jurkovič](/wiki/Ivan_Jurkovi%C4%8D "Ivan Jurkovič") and [Mitja Leskovar](/wiki/Mitja_Leskovar "Mitja Leskovar") serve in the Holy See's diplomatic service. Jurkovič has served as [Apostolic Nuncio](/wiki/Apostolic_Nuncio "Apostolic Nuncio") to [Canada](/wiki/Apostolic_Nunciature_to_Canada "Apostolic Nunciature to Canada") since 2021, and Leskovar has served as [Apostolic Nuncio](/wiki/Apostolic_Nuncio "Apostolic Nuncio") to [Democratic Republic of the Congo](/wiki/Apostolic_Nunciature_to_the_Democratic_Republic_of_the_Congo "Apostolic Nunciature to the Democratic Republic of the Congo") since 2024.
